observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following:
a. Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs, prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team; and
b. The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile and update their respective PPE records, and make correcting entries for noted discrepancies.
We also recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive create a special committee to handle the procedural requirements on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided for under the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a. The General Services Office designate to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) upon inspection of properties considered unserviceable, and furnish the Accounting Office with a copy of the report; b. The General Services Office designate to request in writing for the disposal of the unserviceable properties, to the Office of the Auditor, upon submission of the IIRUP in accordance with Section 167 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 22, 1992; and c. The Municipal Accountant, thereafter, to prepare necessary journal entries to derecognize from the books of account all the disposed unserviceable properties to reflect the correct balance of the recorded PPE.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive designate an official/employee responsible for consolidating the data from each department, and to prepare and submit the Report on Government PPA to the Audit Team within the prescribed period. We also recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to prepare the list of all ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPAs and to submit the same to the designated official/employee for consolidation.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the following: a. Status Report of the Fund Transfers of the Municipality as of December 31, 2022 and the Status of Implementation of Projects and other documentary evidence; and b. Subsidiary ledgers of the accounts under Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs as of December 31, 2022.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to do the following: 1. Reconcile their records and make the appropriate adjustment to reflect the correct balances of the RPT/SET receivables; and 2. Strictly comply with the procedures provided under the NGAS Manual for LGU in the accounting of receivables by preparing and submitting the summary of collections on a weekly basis.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following: 1. Municipal Accountant to record the purchases and issuance/utilization of goods and supplies in its appropriate accounts in order to reflect the correct balances at year-end; and 2. Inventory Committee to properly maintain inventory records for monitoring and record-keeping purposes, conduct the semestral physical count on inventories, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare subsidiary records duly counter-checked by each office to prove that reconciliation is regularly done and to easily identify the source of error or discrepancies in the reports.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to regularly do the following: a. Prepare and submit to the COA Office Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Establish the SWM Task Force or appoint personnel to impose the fees, fines and penalties due to the LGU as embodied in Ordinance No. 2019-006 dated September 26, 2019 entitled “Anti Littering Ordinance of the Municipality of Balasan”, and as provided in Section 47 and 49 of the Act; and 2. (further actions)

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct: 1. The persons responsible to comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 especially on addressing the client and organization focused gender issues; 2. The MSWDO and Budget Officer to observe proper charging of expenses against the GAD Fund; 3. The MSWDO to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report in the proper form required complete with the required attachments including the HGDG tools or PIMME Checklist to support the attributed cost of the Municipal Government’s regular Projects/Program/Activities to GAD; and 4. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents are complete before payment and transmittal to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and management agreed that: 1. The MDRRMO to see to it that all programs, projects and activities to be funded under the 70 percent Mitigation Fund are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready; and 2. The MDRRMO re-visit the previous years’ LDRRM Annual Investment Plan, check the unimplemented programs and PPAs. Still with unimplemented projects that remained unimplemented as of CY 2021 if it is still doable and necessary or if it needs to be re-allocated to more responsive programs and projects.

The Local School Board to comply strictly with the provisions of the Budget Operations Manual specifically on the Budget Process for the SEF; The persons responsible for these disbursements to refund those disbursements found to have no corresponding appropriation, otherwise audit suspensions will be issued.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each existing stallholder for monitoring and record-keeping purposes; and
2. Make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the amount receivable from the market stallholders at year end.

We recommended that the LCE direct the responsible/concerned department heads to do the following (based on the guidelines prescribed by COA Circular No. 2018-002): 1. Secure directly from the GSIS GIF, all insurances or bonds covering properties, contracts, rights of action and other insurable risks of their respective offices; 2. Prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF) listing of the insurable properties and other assets, showing their latest appraised values/valuation, appraised date, location, and other information; 3. Extract from the Report on the Physical Count of PPE the data for the PIF pertaining to the insurable assets and interest of the government; 4. Cause the appraisal of the insurable properties and other assets of their respective offices; 5. Submit the consolidated PIF to the Office of the Auditor and the GIF, GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year; 6. Include in the annual appropriation the amount of premiums for the general insurance covering all insurable assets and other assets and ensure its payment to the GSIS; and 7. Ensure centralized payment of insurance premiums to avoid double payment.

After the legal creation of the LEE, the following provisions shall be complied pursuant to Section 5.1 of the Manual: a. For the next budget period, exclude the salaries, wages, representation, and transportation allowances of LEE officials and employees from the GF budget. b. Charge the appropriations for personal services of the LEEs against its own Annual Operating Budget (AOB). c. Exclude the salaries, wages, and allowances of the LEEs in the computation of the maximum amount of PS of the Municipality. d. Ensure that no LEE official or employee is allowed a salary rate higher that the maximum fixed for his position or other positions of equivalent rank. e. Check that each LEE has its own separate AOB. f. Include the PPAs of LEEs in the Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) and Annual Investment Program (AIP). g. Apply the profits or income derived from the operations of LEEs to: • First, cost of improvement, repair, and related expenses of the LEE; and • Second, return advances received, if any, by the LEEs to the GF. • Any excess shall form part of the GF. h. Ensure that LEE AOB is included in the approved appropriation ordinance of the General Fund Annual Budget.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adhere strictly to the maximum allowable PS cost that the LGU may incur annually. We also recommend that he require the officials and employees concerned to refund the benefits claimed.

1.8. We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct a
proper inventory taking and reconcile the PPE accounts reported in the
FS and in the RPCPPE and make necessary corrections and adjustments
on the discrepancies noted.

1.9. We also recommended and the GSO agreed to maintain the complete
Property Cards for proper reconciliation with the Accounting Office’s
Property Ledger Cards.

1.10. Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed to avail of the
one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to finally establish accurate and reliable
PPE balances.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to implement the Perpetual Inventory Method in
the accounting of inventories in compliance with Section 114 of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for
LGUs Volume I;
2. GSO to ensure that the issuance of all supplies and materials is
supported with Requisition and Issue Slips and that the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued is prepared and submitted to the
Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense; and
3. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain the required Supplies
Ledger Cards (SLC) and Stock Cards, respectively.

3.8. We recommended, and the Management agreed to the following:
a. The Municipal Treasurer provides the Municipal Accountant with a
duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due
and collectible for the year, to serve as the basis for setting up RPT
and SET Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year;
b. The Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to record a debit
to RPT/SET Receivables and credit to Deferred RPT/SET Income
based on the certified list of due and collectible amounts submitted by
the Municipal Treasurer; and
c. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant regularly
reconcile the records related to RPT/SET transactions to ensure
accuracy, making the necessary corrections/adjustments to records as
needed.

4.10. We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
1. Exert efforts to retrieve the details and supporting documents of the
outstanding payables, and review and analyze the same to determine
valid claims; and
2. Prepare and issue confirmation letter/s for payables with details and
identified creditors or claimants, and require the immediate
submission of the necessary supporting documents to facilitate
payment of the accounts.

5.10. We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Undertake measures in identifying the undetermined prior year’s
balances of the account Due to NGAs; and
b. Determine the unexpended balances of fully implemented fund
transfers and facilitate their return to their respective source agencies.

6.7. We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the following
actions:
a. The Acting-Municipal Treasurer to verify the status of stale checks
amounting to ₱44,100.92 and request for revision, if warranted; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to revert the stale checks as of year-end
based on the status provided by the Acting Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002 by effecting the necessary adjustments and ensuring that henceforth, unexpended balances of the LDRRMF at year end shall be booked under Trust Liability – DRRM.

Thus, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to review the entries made to record the transactions funded under the development fund and make necessary and correcting entries as necessary and henceforth, observe recording of transactions to appropriate accounts for fair presentation.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Prepare and provide a copy of the list of unreleased checks to the Municipal Accountant at the end of the year; and
b. Monitor long outstanding checks, and send out notices to the payee of the check at least one month before the check becomes stale.
We also recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Ensure that only valid checks issued and released are reported as reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements based on the report submitted by the Municipal Treasurer; and
b. Make the necessary adjustments for unreleased checks at year-end, to restore the correct Cash in Bank and in the corresponding Payable account balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and management agreed that they will:
a. Revisit all unimplemented programs/projects/activities funded under the 20% Development Fund, identify impediments and deterrents affecting the implementation of planned PPAs and develop solutions to address such issues or consider those PPAs that are no longer doable to be replaced with development projects which are well-planned, procurement and implementation-ready and properly coordinated with the concerned departments; and
b. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office, and regularly monitor the implementation of the targeted development projects funded out of 20% DF; and
c. Ensure that the utilization of the 20% Development Fund is maximized in order that the target beneficiaries could timely avail of the benefits and use of the projects.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Effect the necessary entries to reflect the completed asset in the general fund books and to arrive at the correct balance of the trust liabilities account; and
b. Closely monitor the expenditures to avoid cash insufficiency and see to it that monies held in trust be used solely for the intended purpose, in accordance with Section 305 (e) of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to do the following:
a. Submit the lacking documents mentioned above such as the program of activities, office order of resource persons and participants, and those documents identified in the Annexes to the audit team; and
b. Take note of the documentary requirements for common government transactions as provided for, in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure completeness of supporting documents prior to the payment of disbursement vouchers.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive designate personnel who will consolidate the detailed action plans of each department and at the same time will prepare and submit the AAPSI to the Audit Team; b. The Local Chief Executive direct all Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2022 AAR findings and recommendations including those of prior years to the personnel assigned to consolidate the same; and c. Henceforth, the Municipal Government, within sixty (60) days from its receipt of the Annual Audit Report, submit to the Audit Team, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations stated therein.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the: 1. MDRRM Officer and Other Persons Responsible to: a. Ensure that expenses that are administrative in nature shall be charged against the appropriations for the MDRRM Office and use the fund prudently; Municipal Accountant to double check completeness and propriety of supporting documents before payment to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.
Provide the Municipal Accountant data e.g., list of market stall occupants, corresponding rental rate, the amount due from them as at December 31, 2019, and payments made thereafter.

1. The Acting Municipal Treasurer to: • See to it that Collections on hand are deposited daily or not later than the next banking day; • Issue demand for those Collectors not with remittance; • Issue letter of authority to the Barangay Treasurer as deputized collector and request prompt turnover of collections to the Designated Liquidating Officer; and • Formulate policies and procedures in the issuance of the accountable forms to the collectors, period of remittance in accordance with the Local Treasury Operations Manual and proper reporting of collections/remittance/deposits and accountable forms received and issued in the Reports of Collections and Deposits/Remittances. 2. The Collectors to turnover collections on hand at the close of each business day to the Liquidating Officer and observe issuance of pre-numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence; and 3. The Municipal Accountant to record the under remittance of collections in the books of accounts as due from the concerned accountable officer.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to start maintaining the subsidiary ledgers not only for cash but for all accounts of the Municipality. We further recommended for the monthly reconciliation of the subsidiary ledgers and the cash books of the accountable officers by the Municipal Accountant of the certification of balances as prescribed by the Local Treasury Operations Manual stated below for ready reference. Item F, Section 63 of the LTOM provides that all accountable officers who are required to maintain a cashbook shall reconcile his cashbook balances every end of the month or as the need arises with the subsidiary accounting records. A certification of balances as appearing in the subsidiary accounting records shall be obtained from the Accountant at the end of the month or as required by competent authority.

1. Direct the preparation of a 5-Year Staffing Plan pursuant to Section 5.2 of the Manual; 2. Assign responsible personnel to develop a detailed 5-Year Business Plan (BP) for the LEE. The BP shall cover all facets of the LEE operations including management, operations, staffing, risk analysis, marketing and sales. Appendix A of the same Manual shows a detailed outline for a BP that follows the requirements of commercial and development financing institutions; and 3. Direct the person in-charge to: a. Present the 5-Year Business Plan and stress the importance of directing the LEE operations towards self-sufficiency; and b. Review and assess the LEEs five years after instituting changes to comply with the legal provisions governing such. Review and assessment activities will help the Municipality determine whether to continue, modify, expand, scale down or close the LEE operations. Section 7 of the same Manual provides the guidelines in assessing whether the LEEs are viable and shall continue to operate or not.

After the legal creation of the LEE, the following provisions shall be complied pursuant to Section 5.1 of the Manual:

For the next budget period, exclude the salaries, wages, representation, and transportation allowances of LEE officials and employees from the GF budget.
Charge the appropriations for personal services of the LEEs against its own Annual Operating Budget (AOB).
Exclude the salaries, wages, and allowances of the LEEs in the computation of the maximum amount of PS of the Municipality.
Ensure that no LEE official or employee is allowed a salary rate higher that the maximum fixed for his position or other positions of equivalent rank.
Check that each LEE has its own separate AOB.
Include the PPAs of LEEs in the Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) and Annual Investment Program (AIP).
Apply the profits or income derived from the operations of LEEs to:

First, cost of improvement, repair, and related expenses of the LEE; and
Second, return advances received, if any, by the LEEs to the GF.
Any excess shall form part of the GF.
Ensure that LEE AOB is included in the approved appropriation ordinance of the General Fund Annual Budget.

Recommendations:


   We recommended the following:


   1. The Supply Officer to prepare procedures on the  conduct  of  physical
      inventory of PPE and present such to the Agency Head for approval;


   2. The Inventory Committee,  with  the  Supply  Officer  as  one  of  the
      members, to develop a plan to include  a  timeline  for  the  physical
      inventory, who will supervise the  count,  the  personnel  to  act  as
      counters and checkers, and the sequence of  the  counting.  A  set  of
      written instructions shall be prepared to be observed by  each  person
      connected with  the  inventory-taking.  For  furniture  and  fixtures,
      equipment and the like, inventory tags shall be serially  pre-numbered
      and prepared in duplicate with a perforation separating  the  top  and
      bottom portions of the tag;


   3. The Inventory Committee to  physically  count  all  existing  PPE  and
      provide complete description. Secure other information such as  person
      accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no.,  engine  no.,
      etc. and determine if unserviceable or not;


   4. Based on the information gathered in procedure  no.  3,  determine  if
      accounting records and property records were prepared for these  PPEs.
      If there were none, the Municipal Accountant and  the  Supply  Officer
      shall create Ledger Card (LC) and Property Card (PC) for each  PPE  as
      discussed above. PPE without  accounting  records  shall  be  properly
      disclosed;


   5. For the current purchases of PPE, the  Municipal  Accountant  and  GSO
      shall  prepare  the  PPE  LC  and  PC,  respectively  and  issue   the
      corresponding Acknowledgement  Receipt  for  Equipment  (ARE)  to  the
      accountable employee;


   6. Management to submit the Report on physical inventory as  required  by
      regulations; and


   7. The Inventory Committee shall identify  the  unserviceable  properties
      and recommend it for disposal thru  an  appraisal  report  which  will
      eventually be evaluated by the COA Technical Services.

Recommendations:


   We recommended the following:


   1. The Municipal Assessor to provide a copy of an updated Assessment Roll
      to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to Article 339 of the  IRR  of  RA
      No. 7160 which states that the  Municipal  Assessor  shall  submit  an
      assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties

      Municipal Treasurer concerned on or before the 31st  day  of  December
      each year.

      index card based on the Assessment  Roll  and  furnish  the  Municipal
      Accountant a duly certified list showing the names  of  taxpayers  and
      the amount due and collectible for the year;


   3. The Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the debit to  RPT/SET
      Receivables and  credit  to  Deferred  RPT/SET  Income  based  on  the
      certified list of due and collectible RPT for the  year  submitted  by
      the Municipal Treasurer; and


   4. The Municipal Accountant to  reconcile  recordings  involving  RPT/SET
      transactions and make necessary correcting/adjusting entries.

   3. Establish the validity of the P4.833 million Terminal  Leave  Benefits
      Expenses as presented in the Financial Statements;


   4. Submit the General Ledger to the Office of the Auditor of the Terminal
      Leave Benefits Expenses; and


   5. Make the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted.

Recommendations:

   1. Municipal Development Council to:


      • Include in the  proposed  Annual  Investment  Plan  those  projects
        specifically enumerated in  the  DILG-DBM  JMC  Nos.  2017-1  dated

        not fully implemented as of year-end. Assess those projects if  the
        same are implementable and if not, re-align the said funds to other
        programs/projects/activities  that  are  needed  in  this  time  of
        pandemic; and


      • Require the MPDO to submit feasibility study,  project  development
        study and other pre-implementation activities in order to assess if
        the  proposed   programs/projects/activities   are   feasible   and
        implementable.  The  budget  of  which  shall  be  charged  to  the
        concerned Office.


   2. Municipal Accountant to:


      • Strictly examine  the  completeness  of  the  supporting  documents
        before certifying in the Box A of the Disbursement Voucher (DV);

        related supporting documents of  expenses  charged  under  the  20%
        Development Fund (DF). Audit suspensions or disallowances  will  be
        issued if none was submitted thereafter; and


      • Coordinate with  the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  relative  to  the
        charges  of  the  disbursements  under  the  20%  DF   and   submit

   3. Concerned Officials and  Personnel  to  avoid  charging  non-allowable
      expenses  and  disbursements  that  do  not  partake  the  nature   of
      investments or capital  expenditures.  Audit  suspension/disallowances
      will be issued for all disbursements found to be irregular.


   4. The Bids and Awards Committee to fully comply with RA No. 9184 and the
      Philippine  Bidding  Documents  for  Procurement   of   Infrastructure
      Projects.


   5. The Municipal Budget Officer to:


      • Ensure that appropriation for a  specific  project  shall  be  used
        solely for the implementation of the said project;


      • Avoid charging non-related expenses against the 20% DF; and


      • Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant relative to the charges of
        the disbursements under the 20% DF and submit  explanation  on  the
        deficiencies noted above.

     a. Ensure that the administrative expenses shall  be  charged  against

        issued if none is submitted;

     d. Submit the lacking supporting documents of the submitted DVs.  Audit
        suspensions or disallowances will be issued if none  is  submitted;
        and


     e. Inform the Chairman and Board of Directors  of  the  Balasan  Water
        District  to  refund  all  of  their  claims  from  the   Municipal
        Government that were charged against the LDRRMF. Submit copy of the
        settlements to the Office of the Auditor.  Audit  suspensions  or
        disallowances will be issued if none is submitted;


   2. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and LDRRMO to reconcile
      their respective records relative to the disbursements  charged  under
      the LDRRMF; and


   3. Municipal Accountant to double check  completeness  and  propriety  of
      supporting documents before payment.

      • Ensure that the supporting documents are  complete  before  payment

   1.  Execute  lease  contracts  with  market   stallholders   to   enhance

   2. Prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF)  listing  of  the  insurable

   4. Cause the appraisal of the insurable properties and  other  assets  of

   5. Submit the consolidated PIF to the Office of the Auditor and the  GIF,

   6. Include in the annual appropriation the amount  of  premiums  for  the
      general insurance covering all insurable assets and other  assets  and

   7. Ensure centralized payment  of  insurance  premiums  to  avoid  double

Recommendation:

   We recommended that  the  LCE  direct  the  person  in-charge  to  submit

   We also recommended that  henceforth,  the  Municipal  Government,  shall

   recommendations.

   2. Augment the Accounting Unit, if needed, with  competent  personnel  to

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:


   1. The Supply Officer to prepare procedures on the  conduct  of  physical
      inventory of PPE and present such to the Agency Head for approval;


   2. The Inventory Committee,  with  the  Supply  Officer  as  one  of  the
      members, to develop a plan to include  a  timeline  for  the  physical
      inventory, who will supervise the  count,  the  personnel  to  act  as
      counters and checkers, and the sequence of  the  counting.  A  set  of
      written instructions shall be prepared to be observed by  each  person
      connected with  the  inventory-taking.  For  furniture  and  fixtures,
      equipment and the like, inventory tags shall be serially  pre-numbered
      and prepared in duplicate with a perforation separating  the  top  and
      bottom portions of the tag;


   3. The Inventory Committee to  physically  count  all  existing  PPE  and
      provide complete description. Secure other information such as  person
      accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no.,  engine  no.,
      etc. and determine if unserviceable or not;


   4. Based on the information gathered in procedure  no.  3,  determine  if
      accounting records and property records were prepared for these  PPEs.
      If there were none, the Municipal Accountant and  the  Supply  Officer
      shall create Ledger Card (LC) and Property Card (PC) for each  PPE  as
      discussed above. PPE without  accounting  records  shall  be  properly
      disclosed;


   5. For the current purchases of PPE, the  Municipal  Accountant  and  GSO
      shall  prepare  the  PPE  LC  and  PC,  respectively  and  issue   the
      corresponding Acknowledgement  Receipt  for  Equipment  (ARE)  to  the
      accountable employee; and


   6. Management to submit the Report on physical inventory as  required  by
      regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting
   entries to recognize the parcels of land in the book of accounts based on
   the data gathered by the Appraisal Committee.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

   1. Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of the  local  roads
      under the jurisdiction of the Municipality, and prepare the Report  on
      the Physical Count  of  the  Road  Network  System  (Annex  C  of  the
      Circular).


   2. General Services Officer to do following:


     a. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network
        Property Card for all roads and its  components  (Annex  E  of  the
        Circular); and
     b. Render a Report on the Local Road Network (Annex A of the Circular)
        at the end of the accounting period.

   3. Municipal Accountant to do the following:


     a. Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the balance of the local road
        network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road
        Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), and support it  with
        the Registry of Public Infrastructure for Roads and working papers;

     b. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road  components
        for every road network;

     c. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the  depreciation
        for each component at the end of the year; and

     d. Disclose in the Notes to FS the total road network system (Annex  B
        of the Circular); and


   4. Municipal Engineer to provide the local  accountant  and  the  general
      services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of
      road components for road projects.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

   1. Persons/s responsible to devise a plan on how to improve  the  quality
      of the water and make it potable or suitable for drinking,  prepare  a
      proposal relative to the reasonable fees/charges to be charged for the
      use of the facilities, submit the same for approval  of  the  LCE  and
      include in the proposed Revised Revenue Code;



   2. Assignment of person/s responsible to facilitate the  organization  of
      communities as water users or as operator of the community based water
      system  and  formulate  strategies  in  managing  and  sustaining  the
      operations of the project; and



   3. Designation of a permanent employee to be responsible for  the  safety
      of the properties, in-charge in maintaining  the  cleanliness  of  the
      surroundings and  be  liable  in  case  of  loss  or  damage  to  said
      properties.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Municipal Planning and Development Council  to  only  include  in  the
      proposed budget and Annual Investment Plan, those  programs,  projects
      and activities that are enumerated in Section 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC  No.
      2017-1  and  shall  partake  the  nature  of  investment  or   capital
      expenditures; and


   2. Concerned officials and personnel to disburse the funds in  accordance
      with the pertinent provisions of DILG-DBM JMC  No.  2017-1  and  avoid
      charging non-allowable expenses provided in  Section  4.0  thereof  in
      order to avoid future suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following꞉


   1. MDRRM Officer and Other  Persons  Responsible  to  draft  a  budgetary
      proposal for the appropriation of MOOE and capital outlay of the MDRRM
      Office and avoid charging the same to the 5% MDRRM Fund  in  order  to
      avoid future audit suspensions/disallowances;


   2. Persons responsible to attach complete supporting  documents  to  each
      and every claim to ascertain the  correctness  and  propriety  of  the
      same; and



   3. Municipal Accountant to double check  completeness  and  propriety  of
      supporting documents before payment.

We recommended that the LCE together with the concerned personnel:

   1. Fully implement the pertinent provisions of RA  No.  9003,  especially
      the part on waste processing, collection, transportation, grading  and
      compaction, application and maintenance of waste cover, and imposition
      and collection of fees, fines and penalties; and


   2. Eventually  establish  a  sanitary  landfill  which  is  the  ultimate
      requirement in ensuring  the  protection  of  the  public  health  and
      environment of the Municipality as prescribed by RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Office of the Municipal  Treasurer
   to examine the books of accounts and pertinent records of the  businesses
   once every tax period and  comply  with  the  manner  of  examination  as
   prescribed by the Manual.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee  thru  the  LCE  and  the
   Sangguniang Bayan propose the inclusion of fees,  penalties  and  charges
   for the Solid Waste Management and  SALINTUBIG  projects  by  way  of  an
   amendment in the specific provision of  the  Municipal  Revenue  Code  of
   2018.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Persons responsible to comply with  the  provisions  of  PCW-DILG-DBM-
      NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 especially on addressing the  client
      and organization focused gender issues;



   2. MSWDO and Budget  Officer  to  observe  proper  charging  of  expenses
      against the GAD Fund; and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that  the
      supporting documents are complete before payment  and  transmittal  to
      the Office of the Auditor; and



   3. Immediate submission of the duly reviewed and endorsed  GAD  Plan  and
      Budget and Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Creation of an Appraisal Committee composed of the Municipal Assessor,
      Engineer, and Treasurer to facilitate the gathering of necessary  data
      and information regarding the acquisition and to appraise the value of
      the parcels of land owned by the Municipality;


   2. Designation of focal person responsible for assessing the costs to  be
      incurred in the titling of lots and the  inclusion  of  costs  related
      thereto in the Municipality’s budget; and


   3. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards  and  Real
      Property Ledger cards for the  parcels  of  land  in  compliance  with
      Sections 119 and 120 under Chapter 7 of the  Manual  on  the  National
      Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. The Acting Municipal Treasurer to:


      • See to it that Collections on hand are deposited daily or not later
        than the next banking day;
      • Issue demand for those Collectors with under remittance;
      • Issue letter of authority to the Barangay  Treasurer  as  deputized
        collector  and  request  prompt  turnover  of  collections  to  the
        Designated Liquidating Officer; and
      •  Formulate  policies  and  procedures  in  the  issuance   of   the
        accountable forms  to  the  collectors,  period  of  remittance  in
        accordance with the Local Treasury  Operations  Manual  and  proper
        reporting of collections/ remittance/deposits and accountable forms
        received  and  issued   in   the   Reports   of   Collections   and
        Deposits/Remittances.


   2. The Collectors to turnover collections on hand at the  close  of  each
      business day to the Liquidating Officer and observe issuance  of  pre-
      numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence; and


   3. The Municipal Accountant to record the under remittance of collections
      in the books  of  accounts  as  due  from  the  concerned  accountable
      officer.

We recommended that the LCE direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  start
   maintaining the subsidiary ledgers not only for cash but for all accounts
   of the Municipality.


   We further recommended for the monthly reconciliation of  the  subsidiary
   ledgers and the cash books of the accountable officers  and  issuance  by
   the Municipal Accountant of the certification of balances  as  prescribed
   by the Local Treasury Operations Manual stated below for ready reference.


   Item F, Section 63 of the LTOM provides that all accountable officers who
